source: Curated by Saaksh from recognised Indian regulations and international ESG standards. Principle-level guidance to help consultants suggest practical, credible solutions for each disclosure gap.
note: Best practices are mapped at the BRSR principle level, the most accurate granularity, since leading-practice for a topic (e.g. GHG accounting) is broadly consistent across sectors. Sector-specific nuance is handled in the Materiality Assessment.

P1.name: Ethics, Transparency & Accountability
india: Adopt a board-approved Code of Conduct and a whistle-blower / vigil mechanism (Companies Act 2013 §177 + SEBI LODR Reg. 22) and publish both on the company website.
Constitute the Risk Management and Stakeholders' Relationship Committees mandated for the top 1000 listed entities, with documented charters and meeting records.
Track and disclose anti-corruption training coverage by employee category, as Tata and Infosys do, rather than a simple Yes/No.
international: Certify the anti-bribery programme to ISO 37001 (Anti-Bribery Management Systems) and align with UN Global Compact Principle 10.
Benchmark disclosure against Transparency International's Business Principles for Countering Bribery and publish a standalone Business Ethics report.

P2.name: Sustainable & Safe Goods and Services
india: Conduct Life Cycle Assessments per ISO 14040/14044 for flagship products and pursue BIS Eco-Mark or CII GreenPro certification.
Align packaging and product take-back with the EPR plan filed with the Pollution Control Board; set a recycled-content target.
international: Apply circular-design principles (Ellen MacArthur Foundation) and pursue Cradle-to-Cradle Certified® where relevant.
Adopt a Sustainable Procurement policy to ISO 20400 and set time-bound sustainable-sourcing percentage targets.

P3.name: Employee Wellbeing & Safety
india: Implement an ISO 45001 occupational health & safety management system and ensure POSH Act Internal Committees are constituted and reported.
Disclose Lost Time Injury Frequency Rate (LTIFR) and safety-training coverage, not just incident counts.
international: Commit to the ILO core labour standards and a living-wage benchmark (e.g. Fair Wage Network / Anker methodology).
Run structured engagement and wellbeing programmes and validate with an external survey (e.g. Great Place to Work).

P4.name: Stakeholder Engagement
india: Maintain a stakeholder register that explicitly identifies vulnerable and marginalised groups, and document consultations feeding CSR planning.
Operate a published grievance-redressal mechanism with turnaround-time metrics.
international: Use the AA1000 Stakeholder Engagement Standard for structured, inclusive engagement.
Conduct a double-materiality assessment (impact + financial) to prioritise topics, in line with GRI 3 and ESRS.

P5.name: Human Rights
india: Adopt a Human Rights Policy covering child / forced labour, non-discrimination and freedom of association, and cascade it through a Supplier Code of Conduct.
Ensure documented compliance with POSH, Minimum Wages and Contract Labour regulations, with an accessible grievance channel.
international: Run human-rights due diligence per the UN Guiding Principles on Business & Human Rights (UNGPs) and the OECD Guidelines.
Prepare for the EU Corporate Sustainability Due Diligence Directive (CSDDD) with supply-chain human-rights audits.

P6.name: Environment
india: Implement Zero Liquid Discharge, rainwater harvesting and rooftop solar / renewable PPAs; meet PAT scheme energy-saving targets.
Set a board-approved energy- and water-intensity reduction roadmap with annual milestones.
international: Measure Scope 1, 2 and 3 emissions to the GHG Protocol and set validated Science Based Targets (SBTi).
Operate an ISO 14001 environmental management system and disclose through CDP (Climate / Water).
Move waste up the hierarchy, recycling, co-processing and circular-economy diversion from landfill.

P7.name: Policy Advocacy
india: Disclose all trade-association and industry-body memberships and the public-policy positions advocated through them.
international: Ensure lobbying and advocacy are consistent with the Paris Agreement and net-zero commitments (benchmarked against InfluenceMap-style criteria).
Adopt a Responsible Climate Policy Engagement framework and review trade-body alignment annually.

P8.name: Inclusive Growth & Equitable Development
india: Align CSR spend (Companies Act §135) to the SDGs and conduct Social Impact Assessments for major projects.
Prioritise local procurement and employment, and report beneficiary outcomes, not just rupees spent.
international: Quantify community programmes using Social Return on Investment (SROI) or the B Impact Assessment.
Adopt the principle of Free, Prior and Informed Consent (FPIC) where projects affect local communities.

P9.name: Consumer Responsibility
india: Build data-privacy controls for the Digital Personal Data Protection (DPDP) Act 2023 and comply with the Consumer Protection Act 2019, including transparent labelling.
Operate a customer grievance-redressal system and disclose resolution rates.
international: Certify information security to ISO/IEC 27001 and adopt privacy-by-design.
Run a product-safety / stewardship management system and track customer-satisfaction metrics.
